hook_type: Stakes-named loss-frame opener, 'until X, this was for Y'
tactic: Empathy lead -> first-person community proof -> recursion punch
primary_text:
Until 2018, backing the next big Aussie brand was for VCs and family offices.

Then Crowd-Sourced Funding came in, and we built the rails. 324 raises. $234M+. 137,000 retail investors. Naked Life. Our Cow. Earthletica. Aquafab.

Now we're opening our own raise on the platform we built.

Save your spot. We will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: What VCs got, you can get too
description: 324 raises. $234M+. 137,000 retail investors. Save your spot.
_source_label: DRC V2 -- A3 Empathy + Access + Recursion (first-timers)
_v2_changes: Subject discipline: 'we built the rails' (Birchal-the-company built the platform). 'Our own raise on the platform we built' is unambiguous: same Birchal opens its raise on its own platform.
Cut 'Eight years later, 324 Australian companies have raised on Birchal' -- redundant with the cadence-of-three that follows.
Cut 'Australians who used to be locked out of early-stage equity, owning pieces of brands they believed in' -- flabby clause that rephrases the opener.
_drc_playbook: 03 Aspirational Identity Sell (B2C considered, solution-aware, sophistication 3-4)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors who feel locked out of startup equity.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'.
BRAND-EXCLUDED: only verified-good raises (Naked Life, Our Cow, Earthletica, Aquafab).
